entities.name: SEED DRYING
entities.description: The process of removing excess moisture from seeds to reduce respiration and deterioration while maintaining viability

entities.name: MOISTURE CONTENT
entities.description: The amount of water present in seeds, which is reduced during the drying process

entities.name: RESPIRATION
entities.description: The biological process in seeds that is reduced by the removal of excess moisture

entities.name: DETERIORATION
entities.description: The decline in seed quality that is reduced by seed drying

entities.name: VIABILITY
entities.description: The ability of a seed to germinate, which must be preserved during the drying process

entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: Cicer arietinum, a crop characterized by orthodox seed nature

entities.name: ORTHODOX SEED NATURE
entities.description: A seed classification based on behavior before and during storage, allowing safe drying to very low moisture contents

entities.name: STORAGE
entities.description: The state or period of keeping seeds, which defines the orthodox nature of the seed

entities.name: WATER POTENTIAL
entities.description: A measure of water energy status in seeds, equivalent to specific moisture content levels
